rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH CHUNG VS. BANK OF AMERICA, N.A. et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANTS BANK OF AMERICA and FEDERAL HOME LOAN MORTGAGE CORPORATION's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. COAs 2, 8, & 11 are SUSTAINED without leave to amend. A demurrer to these COAs was previously sustained with leave to amend to state facts showing more than a typical lender-borrower relationship. Plaintiff's amendments do not allege facts sufficient to show a duty of care to Defendant. COA 7, for fraud by Freddie Mac, is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to plead facts, not conclusory statements, proving detrimental reliance, as well as to plead the authority of the various individuals to speak on behalf of the corporation. COA 13, for forcible eviction, is OVERRULED. Complaint states sufficient facts showing threats and physical acts by Freddie Mac to constitute a claim. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FERGUS O'SULLIVAN VS. MICHAEL ROUPPET et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANT MICHAEL ROUPPET's Motion To Vacate Default: OFF CALENDAR based on untimely notice. Notice must be given 16 court days in advance of the hearing date. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: HOWARD L. WESTON, AS TRUSTEE OF THE HOWARD L. VS. TOMMY K ABIB et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 3: DEFENDANT SAID SAOUAF'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD: GRANTED. Motion is unopposed, and good cause is shown. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE KIHAGI VS. KELLY KIMBALL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT KELLY KIMBALL'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. The court finds Defendant, as prevailing party on a contract, is entitled to attorneys' fees. However, the court finds the $29,916.00 in fees requested to be unsupported by any argument and egregiously unreasonable. The defect in the 60-day notice could have been disposed of via a Motion to Quash or a Demurrer. It was not reasonable of Defendant to instead bill 92 hours of 3 attorneys' time to litigate the case all the way to a Motion for Summary Judgment when much cheaper alternatives existed. The Court awards Defendants $5925 in fees, which represents 15 hours of work at the hourly rate of Defendant's most expensive lawyer, and is a reasonable fee for the amount of work that would have been required to dispose of the case via a Motion to Quash or Demurrer. Demurrer is SUSTAINED as to 1st COA without leave to amend. FAC was sustained with leave to amend to include tender or information on why tender should not be required, but P failed to make the amendment. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: AJLOUN ENTERPRISES, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. GLEN LEIS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Ihsan Zghoul
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 5: DEFENDANT GLEN LEIS' Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Ihsan Zghoul: HEARING REQUIRED (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MCCORMACK BARON RAGAN MGMT. SERVICES, INC. VS. MARGARITA HERRERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, Line 6: DEFENDANT MARGARITA HERRERA'S MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES: GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. The court finds Defendants to be the prevailing party. The court GRANTS $3,575 in attorneys fees for 13 hours of work by attorney Irina Naduhovskaya. The court DENIES the request for $6,736.00 for 42.1 hours of work by volunteer third-year law student Alexander Quest. Moving party fails to provide any authority supporting an award of fees for a volunteer law student. (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: RINKAL PATEL VS. MICHAEL SCHMIDT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Deft And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, line 8. PLAINTIFF RINKAL PATEL's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant And Request For Monetary Sanctions GRANTED. Discovery responses are to be served personally on Plaintiff's counsel by August 22, 2014. All matters requested are deemed admitted based on Defendant's failure to respond. See Code of Civil Procedure 2033. Sanctions of $945 are GRANTED. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EMILY KO VS. CARLOS BONILLA et al

rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT CARLOS BONILLA's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain the burden. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DANGER PANDA, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. LAMAR WALENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Monday, August 18, 2014, line 10. DEFENDANTs LAMAR WALENT and SCOTT BEATY's Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Complaint DENIED. Whether Plaintiff unreasonably refused to consent to a sublet of the premises is a question of fact not resolvable via a motion to quash. See Cohen v. Ratinoff(1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 321, 330. Moving party cites no authority for its proposition that a 3-day Notice to Quit must specifically allege the landlord has not unreasonably withheld consent to sublet. = (501/REQ)
